title: Micron results and Qualcomm deal boost global equities
summary: Strong Micron figures and Qualcomm's chip supply to Meta spark a rally in the tech sector and lift major stock indices.

title: Oil retreats to pre-Iran conflict levels, falling below $70
summary: Stabilization of Gulf flows and rising supply push crude to prices not seen since before the military escalation.

title: Gold sinks below $4,000 for the first time in months
summary: The strong dollar and the prospect of high interest rates continue to punish the precious metal, which breaks the $4,000 support level.

title: EasyJet rejects a €4.9 billion takeover bid and negotiates with Castlelake
summary: The low-cost airline confirms it has rejected a takeover offer and is holding alternative talks with the Castlelake fund.

title: Arteche plummets on stock market after placing €100 million in an accelerated offering
summary: The Spanish company completes an accelerated share placement that triggers a sharp drop in its share price.

title: 3i Group soars after reporting 3.3% sales growth at Action
summary: The private equity holding sees its shares rise after publishing strong data from its Action store chain.

title: Volkswagen sells its marine engine division to Bain Capital for €7.4 billion
summary: The German automotive group divests 51% of its marine engine subsidiary in a deal valued at ¥1.4 trillion.

title: Major US banks pass stress tests with potential losses of $700 billion
summary: The Fed publishes stress test results, showing that the largest banks could withstand a severe economic scenario.

title: Bitcoin falls to two-week lows and nears its lowest levels in 20 months
summary: The cryptocurrency continues its downward trend due to deteriorating market sentiment and regulatory uncertainties.

title: German DAX trades flat after positive opening driven by tech stocks
summary: The German index takes a breather after early gains, with an eye on the next resistance levels.

title: Ibex 35 trims gains and closes nearly flat at 19,406 points
summary: The Spanish index moderates its advance during the session and ends with a slight gain of 0.08%.

title: Rheinmetall suffers historic drop, dragging down the European defense sector
summary: The German arms manufacturer plummets on the stock market, infecting other sector companies on fears of military spending cuts.

title: SpaceX issues bonds with demand quadrupling the supply
summary: The aerospace company places debt with demand four times higher than the amount issued, reflecting strong investor interest.

title: Japan plans to invest $2.3 trillion in AI and semiconductors over the next 14 years
summary: The Japanese government announces an ambitious strategic plan to boost artificial intelligence and chip manufacturing.

title: Two leading AI researchers leave Google to join Anthropic
summary: The AI startup Anthropic continues to attract top-tier talent, this time from the tech giant.

title: China detains two Japanese nationals accused of smuggling rare earths
summary: Chinese authorities arrest two Japanese citizens on suspicion of illegal extraction and export of strategic minerals.

title: Trump refuses to sign affordable housing bill
summary: The US president decides not to enact the housing affordability law, leaving the initiative in limbo.

title: Binance leaves Greece and will seek MiCA license in another European country
summary: The cryptocurrency exchange withdraws from the Greek market and announces it will seek authorization under the European regulatory framework.

title: Euro hits lowest level in a year against the dollar
summary: The single currency falls to yearly lows on expectations that the Fed will keep rates high while the ECB could cut them.

title: Santander improves its credit outlook and nears best rating since 2012
summary: The Spanish banking institution sees its rating strengthened thanks to the contribution of Webster and TSB.
